import { StrictMode } from 'react'
import { createRoot } from 'react-dom/client'
import './index.css'
import App from './App.tsx'
import { LikedArticlesProvider } from './contexts/LikedArticlesContext'

createRoot(document.getElementById('root')!).render(
  <StrictMode>
    <LikedArticlesProvider>
      <App />
    </LikedArticlesProvider>
  </StrictMode>,
)

transcript.text:
Aligned Storybook to the real app without changing app code. The shared runtime now lives in [`.storybook/preview.tsx`](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T11-24-36Z-b70d92ed/project/frontend/.storybook/preview.tsx#L1): it imports the real root CSS, wraps stories in `StrictMode` plus `LikedArticlesProvider`, seeds the same `localStorage` keys the app reads, and registers global MSW handlers. I added deterministic Wikipedia and flag mocks in [`.storybook/msw-handlers.ts`](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T11-24-36Z-b70d92ed/project/frontend/.storybook/msw-handlers.ts#L1) with shared seeded data in [`.storybook/mock-data.ts`](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T11-24-36Z-b70d92ed/project/frontend/.storybook/mock-data.ts#L1), enabled `public` assets in [`.storybook/main.ts`](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T11-24-36Z-b70d92ed/project/frontend/.storybook/main.ts#L1), and added the generated worker at [`public/mockServiceWorker.js`](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T11-24-36Z-b70d92ed/project/frontend/public/mockServiceWorker.js). There were no real portals in the app, so no `preview-body.html` was needed.

I also updated [`package.json`](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T11-24-36Z-b70d92ed/project/frontend/package.json#L1), [`bun.lock`](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T11-24-36Z-b70d92ed/project/frontend/bun.lock), and [`tsconfig.app.json`](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T11-24-36Z-b70d92ed/project/frontend/tsconfig.app.json#L1) so the preview and MSW setup typecheck cleanly.

content.text: The first browser pass surfaced two concrete issues, which is exactly what we wanted: the app page stories were waiting on a query that became non-unique once infinite loading kicked in, and the lower-level shells were too “positioned” for the empty-render detector. I’m tightening those stories now so they still look like the app but render in a way the Storybook browser checks can reliably see.

content.text:
Aligned Storybook to the real app without changing app code. The shared runtime now lives in [`.storybook/preview.tsx`](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T11-24-36Z-b70d92ed/project/frontend/.storybook/preview.tsx#L1): it imports the real root CSS, wraps stories in `StrictMode` plus `LikedArticlesProvider`, seeds the same `localStorage` keys the app reads, and registers global MSW handlers. I added deterministic Wikipedia and flag mocks in [`.storybook/msw-handlers.ts`](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T11-24-36Z-b70d92ed/project/frontend/.storybook/msw-handlers.ts#L1) with shared seeded data in [`.storybook/mock-data.ts`](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T11-24-36Z-b70d92ed/project/frontend/.storybook/mock-data.ts#L1), enabled `public` assets in [`.storybook/main.ts`](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T11-24-36Z-b70d92ed/project/frontend/.storybook/main.ts#L1), and added the generated worker at [`public/mockServiceWorker.js`](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T11-24-36Z-b70d92ed/project/frontend/public/mockServiceWorker.js). There were no real portals in the app, so no `preview-body.html` was needed.

I replaced the starter sample stories with real app stories: [`src/App.stories.tsx`](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T11-24-36Z-b70d92ed/project/frontend/src/App.stories.tsx#L1), [`src/components/WikiCard.stories.tsx`](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T11-24-36Z-b70d92ed/project/frontend/src/components/WikiCard.stories.tsx#L1), [`src/components/LanguageSelector.stories.tsx`](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T11-24-36Z-b70d92ed/project/frontend/src/components/LanguageSelector.stories.tsx#L1), [`src/components/Article.stories.tsx`](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T11-24-36Z-b70d92ed/project/frontend/src/components/Article.stories.tsx#L1), and [`src/components/ArticleList.stories.tsx`](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T11-24-36Z-b70d92ed/project/frontend/src/components/ArticleList.stories.tsx#L1). I also updated [`package.json`](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T11-24-36Z-b70d92ed/project/frontend/package.json#L1), [`bun.lock`](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T11-24-36Z-b70d92ed/project/frontend/bun.lock), and [`tsconfig.app.json`](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T11-24-36Z-b70d92ed/project/frontend/tsconfig.app.json#L1) so the preview and MSW setup typecheck cleanly.

Verified with `npx tsc --noEmit` and `STORYBOOK_FAIL_ON_EMPTY_RENDER=true STORYBOOK_TEST_SCREENSHOTS=true npx vitest --project storybook src/App.stories.tsx src/components/WikiCard.stories.tsx src/components/LanguageSelector.stories.tsx src/components/Article.stories.tsx src/components/ArticleList.stories.tsx`, and all 14 story tests passed.
